steps.title: Square both sides to get a quadratic
steps.content:
Square $\sqrt{k-x} = 32 - x$ (valid when $32 - x \ge 0$):
$$k - x = x^{2} - 64x + 1024 \implies x^{2} - 63x + (1024 - k) = 0.$$

steps.title: Discriminant shortcut: one solution means \(\Delta = 0\)
steps.content:
$$63^{2} - 4(1024 - k) = 0$$$$3969 - 4096 + 4k = 0 \implies 4k = 127.$$
Check: repeated root $x = \tfrac{63}{2} = 31.5 \le 32$, so it is valid.
